brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is Hawking radiation from primordial black holes (spin-dependent emission flux) heating interstellar dust, folded through dark-matter halo density profiles to bound the PBH fraction; Paper B's machinery is Rayleigh-Jeans thermalization and constraint-driven condensation of coupled nonlinear oscillators (conserving energy and norm) reproducing Lorenz/Pareto vote distributions. The only overlap is generic thermal vocabulary ('heating'/'thermal'/'thermodynamic'); the governing processes -- radiative emission-plus-dust-thermal-balance versus RJ wave-condensation of an oscillator ensemble -- are different, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
